remember: Students will be able to recall the plot and key events of the poem 'The Interview' by A.K. Ramanujan.
Students will be able to identify and explain the central theme of the poem.
Students will be able to recognize and explain the poet's use of imagery and symbolism.
understand: Students will be able to analyze and explain the poet's use of language and tone to convey the theme.
Students will be able to understand the cultural and historical context in which the poem was written.
Students will be able to explain the poet's use of imagery and symbolism to represent the relationship between the interviewer and the interviewee.
apply: Students will be able to analyze and interpret the poem to understand its relevance to contemporary social issues.
Students will be able to evaluate the poet's use of language and tone to convey the theme and its impact on the reader.
Students will be able to apply the critical thinking skills to analyze and interpret the poem.
analyze: Students will be able to identify and analyze the use of irony, sarcasm, and other literary devices in the poem.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the poet and the interviewee to understand the theme.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the poem with other literary works to understand its significance and relevance.

ncert_objectives: To understand the poet's use of language and tone to convey the theme of the poem.
To analyze the cultural and historical context in which the poem was written.
To evaluate the poet's use of imagery and symbolism to represent the relationship between the interviewer and the interviewee.
board_exam_objectives: To recall and analyze the plot and key events of the poem 'The Interview' by A.K. Ramanujan.
To identify and explain the central theme of the poem and its relevance to contemporary social issues.
To evaluate the poet's use of language and tone to convey the theme and its impact on the reader.
